Emmanuele, good morning. So, how is it going?
Good morning, Marc. Here is a short update on the dermatology campaign. The first milestone is complete, and we are on track.
Good. How many centers are active?
Eleven are active, and we are rolling it out in four more this month.
And how much progress is there in the private market?
A lot of progress. But we have a few delays in the public market, because there is not much information about the reimbursement yet.
Thank you for telling me. I have some news too: Paris is going to send you two more people in July. Are there any problems you cannot solve alone?
Not yet. So far, so good. On Friday I would like to discuss the delays with you.

Emmanuele, Marc Dubois. In August I am going to ask you one question, and I want you to practice the answer now. The question is: how is it going? Not the plan. The plan I already have. I want the progress. So, before our call on Friday, please send me a short update. Three things only. How many centers are on track, and how many are late. How much progress there is in the private market. And any problem that you are not going to solve alone. Please, do not send me forty slides. I do not have much time, and neither do you. Three lines are enough. Talk to you Friday.

Good morning, everyone. Claire Bennett here, with the weekly update from the global immunology team. First, the good news. The dermatology campaign is on track in eleven countries, and we are rolling it out in four more this month. There is a lot of progress in the private market, and the first milestone is complete. Now the difficult part. We have a few delays in the public market, because there is not much information about the reimbursement decision yet. Some countries are still waiting. So, if you are tracking a campaign in your country, please send me two numbers before Friday: how many centers are active, and how many doctors your team is visiting this month. Are there any questions? Write to me. Thank you, everyone.

Good morning, Marc. Here is the update on the dermatology campaign. The first milestone is complete, and we are on track. We have a lot of progress in the private market: eleven centers are active. But we have a few delays in the public market, because there is not much information about the reimbursement yet. So far, so good.
